title: andropogon nutans subvar. neesii
intro: Andropogon nutans subvar. neesii, commonly known as the Yellow Wild Indigo, is a perennial plant species native to India and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Yellow Wild Indigo include relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its therapeutic properties.
